44. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:\n\n1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late\n2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all\n3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed\n\n### Code Flow Before Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    for each window:\n      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE\n      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n        skip element\n      else:\n        process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE\n    sideOutput(element)\n```\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:\n\n### Changes Made\n\n#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`\n\n**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    // Existing merge loop continues here...\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow After Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    if (isElementLate(element)):  // CHECK EARLY\n      // Skip merge loop, isSkippedElement remains true\n    else:\n      for each window:\n        actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE ONLY IF NOT LATE\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n          skip element\n        else:\n          process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // WILL NOW BE TRUE FOR LATE ELEMENTS\n    sideOutput(element)  // ELEMENT IS PROPERLY SIDE-OUTPUT\n```\n\n## Key Improvements\n1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations\n2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output\n3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output\n4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Scenario\n1. Creates a session window with 10-second gap between elements\n2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness\n3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]\n4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)\n5. Sends a late element (timestamp=8000)\n6. Verifies that the late element appears in the side output stream\n\n### Verification\n- Element at t=8000 with allowed lateness=5000: `8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000` (current watermark) \u2192 **LATE**\n- Late elements are correctly emitted to `lateOutputTag` side output\n- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival\n\n## Impact\n- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows\n- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved\n- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows\n\n## Verification Steps\n1. Compile the changes: `mvn clean compile`\n2. Run the new test: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput`\n3. Run full windowing tests: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest`\n4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:\n\n1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late\n2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all\n3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed\n\n### Code Flow Before Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    for each window:\n      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE\n      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n        skip element\n      else:\n        process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE\n    sideOutput(element)\n```\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:\n\n### Changes Made\n\n#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`\n\n**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    // Existing merge loop continues here...\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow After Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    if (isElementLate(element)):  // CHECK EARLY\n      // Skip merge loop, isSkippedElement remains true\n    else:\n      for each window:\n        actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE ONLY IF NOT LATE\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n          skip element\n        else:\n          process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // WILL NOW BE TRUE FOR LATE ELEMENTS\n    sideOutput(element)  // ELEMENT IS PROPERLY SIDE-OUTPUT\n```\n\n## Key Improvements\n1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations\n2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output\n3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output\n4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Scenario\n1. Creates a session window with 10-second gap between elements\n2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness\n3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]\n4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)\n5. Sends a late element (timestamp=8000)\n6. Verifies that the late element appears in the side output stream\n\n### Verification\n- Element at t=8000 with allowed lateness=5000: `8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000` (current watermark) \u2192 **LATE**\n- Late elements are correctly emitted to `lateOutputTag` side output\n- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival\n\n## Impact\n- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows\n- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved\n- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows\n\n## Verification Steps\n1. Compile the changes: `mvn clean compile`\n2. Run the new test: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput`\n3. Run full windowing tests: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest`\n4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
45. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "new_string": "    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements that are too late are properly sent\n        // to the side output and do NOT trigger merges when using session windows with\n        // allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration.\n        //\n        // Critical scenario: An element that is too late (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n        // arrives and COULD extend an existing session window, but should NOT be processed.\n        // Instead, it must be sent to the side output WITHOUT triggering any merge.\n\n        final int sessionGap = 3; // 3 seconds gap to create new session windows\n        final long allowedLateness = 2000; // 2 seconds in milliseconds\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // === Phase 1: Create and fire a session window ===\n        // Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+3000] = [1000, 4000]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n\n        // Watermark to 5000: triggers window fire (window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 > 5000)\n        // Window fires but is NOT cleaned up yet\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // === Phase 2: Advance watermark to clean up the window ===\n        // Watermark to 7000: now cleanup time = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 <= 7000, so window is cleaned up\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // === Phase 3: Send a late element - critical test ===\n        // Element at t=3000:\n        // - isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = TRUE\n        // - This element would have merged with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]\n        // - But since it's late, it MUST be sent to side output WITHOUT merging\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n\n        // Expected: element goes to side output only, NOT to main output\n        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Late output was not correct.\",\n                expectedLateOutput,\n                lateOutput,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "old_string": "    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements are properly sent to the side output\n        // when using session windows with allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration\n\n        final int sessionGap = 10; // 10 seconds gap between elements to create new session\n        final long allowedLateness = 5; // Allow 5 seconds of lateness\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness * 1000, // Convert to milliseconds\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Create a session window:\n        // Element 1 at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+10000] = [1000, 11000]\n        // Element 2 at t=5000 falls within window [1000, 11000+10000-5000] range, causing merge\n        // Final window is [1000, 15000] (5000 + 10000)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 5000));\n\n        // Advance watermark to 16000\n        // Window maxTimestamp=15000, cleanup time=15000+5000=20000\n        // Since watermark 16000 < cleanup time 20000, window is NOT cleaned up yet\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(16000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 14999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 14999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(16000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // Advance watermark beyond cleanup time (>20000)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(21000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(21000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // Send a late element at t=8000\n        // isElementLate checks: 8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000? YES - it's late!\n        // This late element should be sent to the side output\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));\n\n        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Late output was not correct.\",\n                expectedLateOutput,\n                lateOutput,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java",
  "newString": "    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements that are too late are properly sent\n        // to the side output and do NOT trigger merges when using session windows with\n        // allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration.\n        //\n        // Critical scenario: An element that is too late (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n        // arrives and COULD extend an existing session window, but should NOT be processed.\n        // Instead, it must be sent to the side output WITHOUT triggering any merge.\n\n        final int sessionGap = 3; // 3 seconds gap to create new session windows\n        final long allowedLateness = 2000; // 2 seconds in milliseconds\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness,\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // === Phase 1: Create and fire a session window ===\n        // Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+3000] = [1000, 4000]\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n\n        // Watermark to 5000: triggers window fire (window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 > 5000)\n        // Window fires but is NOT cleaned up yet\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // === Phase 2: Advance watermark to clean up the window ===\n        // Watermark to 7000: now cleanup time = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 <= 7000, so window is cleaned up\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // === Phase 3: Send a late element - critical test ===\n        // Element at t=3000:\n        // - isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = TRUE\n        // - This element would have merged with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]\n        // - But since it's late, it MUST be sent to side output WITHOUT merging\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n\n        // Expected: element goes to side output only, NOT to main output\n        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Late output was not correct.\",\n                expectedLateOutput,\n                lateOutput,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",
  "oldString": "    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements are properly sent to the side output\n        // when using session windows with allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration\n\n        final int sessionGap = 10; // 10 seconds gap between elements to create new session\n        final long allowedLateness = 5; // Allow 5 seconds of lateness\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness * 1000, // Convert to milliseconds\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Create a session window:\n        // Element 1 at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+10000] = [1000, 11000]\n        // Element 2 at t=5000 falls within window [1000, 11000+10000-5000] range, causing merge\n        // Final window is [1000, 15000] (5000 + 10000)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 5000));\n\n        // Advance watermark to 16000\n        // Window maxTimestamp=15000, cleanup time=15000+5000=20000\n        // Since watermark 16000 < cleanup time 20000, window is NOT cleaned up yet\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(16000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 14999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 14999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(16000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // Advance watermark beyond cleanup time (>20000)\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(21000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(21000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n\n        // Send a late element at t=8000\n        // isElementLate checks: 8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000? YES - it's late!\n        // This late element should be sent to the side output\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));\n\n        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Late output was not correct.\",\n                expectedLateOutput,\n                lateOutput,\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }",

limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.OpenContext;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.ReduceFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.serialization.SerializerConfigImpl;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeHint;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperatorFactory;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.watermark.Watermark;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.ProcessingTimeSessionWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.SessionWindowTimeGapExtractor;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.ContinuousEventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.CountTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.EventTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.ProcessingTimeTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.PurgingTrigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.GlobalWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.TimeWindow;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalIterableWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalSingleValueProcessWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalSingleValueWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.KeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.TestHarnessUtil;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.util.asyncprocessing.AsyncKeyedOneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.Collector;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.base.Joiner;\nimport org.apache.flink.shaded.guava33.com.google.common.collect.Iterables;\n\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.params.ParameterizedTest;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.params.provider.ValueSource;\n\nimport java.io.Serializable;\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.Collection;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Comparator;\nimport java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentLinkedQueue;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicInteger;\n\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.mock;\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n\n/** Tests for {@link WindowOperator}. */\nclass WindowOperatorTest {\n\n    private interface HarnessProvider<IN, OUT> {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                create();\n    }\n\n    private static final TypeInformation<Tuple2<String, Integer>> STRING_INT_TUPLE =\n            TypeInformation.of(new TypeHint<Tuple2<String, Integer>>() {});\n\n    // For counting if close() is called the correct number of times on the SumReducer\n    private static AtomicInteger closeCalled = new AtomicInteger(0);\n\n    // late arriving event OutputTag<StreamRecord<IN>>\n    private static final OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>> lateOutputTag =\n            new OutputTag<Tuple2<String, Integer>>(\"late-output\") {};\n\n    private void testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize), Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testSlidingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n        final int windowSlide = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize),\n                                        Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new RichSumReducer<TimeWindow>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        SlidingEventTimeWindows.of(\n                                Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize), Duration.ofSeconds(windowSlide)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testSlidingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    private void testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n            HarnessProvider<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>> harnessProvider)\n            throws Exception {\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 20));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1998));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(1999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = harnessProvider.create();\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 5999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5999));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // those don't have any effect...\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(6999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsReduce(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () ->\n                                createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testTumblingEventTimeWindowsApply(boolean enableAsyncState) throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int windowSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new RichSumReducer<TimeWindow>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n\n        testTumblingEventTimeWindows(\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? () -> createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new RichSumReducer<>()))\n                        : () -> createTestHarness(operator));\n\n        // we close once in the rest...\n        assertThat(closeCalled).as(\"Close was not called.\").hasValue(2);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws Exception 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n              String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                          new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                     new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testReduceSessionWindowsWithProcessFunction() throws                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueProcessWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 5501));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 6), 6050));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-6\", 10L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-20\", 5501L, 9050L), 9049));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 10), 15000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 20), 15000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-30\", 15000L, 18000L), 17999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(17999));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the CountTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithCountTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(CountTrigger.of(4)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-10\", 0L, 6500L), 6499));\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 6000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 6500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 7000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        // add an element that merges the two \"key1\" sessions, they should now have count 6, and\n        // therefore fire\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 10), 4500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-22\", 10L, 10000L), 9999L));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /** This tests whether merging works correctly with the ContinuousEventTimeTrigger. */\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithContinuousEventTimeTrigger() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        final int sessionSize = 3;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                ContinuousEventTimeTrigger.of(Duration.ofSeconds(2)),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        // add elements out-of-order and first trigger time is 2000\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 3), 2500));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 2), 1000));\n\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 4000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-1\", 1500L, 4500L), 4499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-6\", 0L, 5500L), 5499));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(2500));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 5), 4000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(3000));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(3000));\n\n        // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n\n        expectedOutput.clear();\n        testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n        testHarness.setup();\n        testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 4000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 4), 3500));\n        // triggers emit and next trigger time is 6000\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-3\", 1500L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-15\", 0L, 7000L), 6999));\n        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(4000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expectedOutput,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * This tests a custom Session window assigner that assigns some elements to \"point windows\",\n     * windows that have the same timestamp for start and end.\n     *\n     * <p>In this test, elements that have 33 as the second tuple field will be put into a point\n     * window.\n     */\n    @Test\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    void testPointSessions() throws Exception {\n        closeCalled.set(0);\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                new PointSessionWindows(3000),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new SessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                0,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        OperatorSubtaskState snapshot;\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            // add elements out-of-order\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 0));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 1000));\n\n            // do a snapshot, close and restore again\n            snapshot = testHarness.snapshot(0L, 0L);\n        }\n\n        try (OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator)) {\n            testHarness.setup();\n            testHarness.initializeState(snapshot);\n            testHarness.open();\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 10));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 1000));\n            testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 33), 2500));\n\n            testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key1-36\", 10L, 4000L), 3999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-67\", 0L, 3000L), 2999));\n            expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(12000));\n\n            TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                    \"Output was not correct.\",\n                    expectedOutput,\n                    testHarness.getOutput(),\n                    new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements are properly sent to the side output\n        // when using session windows with allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration\n\n        final int sessionGap = 10; // 10 seconds gap between elements to create new session\n        final long allowedLateness = 5; // Allow 5 seconds of lateness\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                allowedLateness * 1000, // Convert to milliseconds\n                                lateOutputTag);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // Create a session window:\n        // Element 1 at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+10000] = [1000, 11000]\n        // Element 2 at t=5000 falls within window [1000, 11000+10000-5000] range, causing merge\n        // Final window is [1000, 15000] (5000 + 10000)\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new
