stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        // because of the small allowed lateness and because the trigger is accumulating\n        // this will be merged into the session (11600-14600) and therefore will not\n        // be sideoutput as late\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n\n        // adding (\"key2\", 1) extended the session to (10000-146000) for which\n        // maxTimestamp <= currentWatermark. Therefore, we immediately get a firing\n        // with the current version of EventTimeTrigger/EventTimeTriggerAccum\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-2\", 10000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-3\", 10000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testNotSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithHugeLatenessPurgingTrigger() throws Exception {\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                PurgingTrigger.of(EventTimeTrigger.create()),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testNotSideOutputDueToLatenessSessionWithHugeLateness() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10000;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                lateOutputTag /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        // this will not be sideoutput because the session we're adding two has maxTimestamp\n        // after the current watermark\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 4500));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 8500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(7400));\n\n        // this will merge the two sessions into one\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 7000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-5\", 1000L, 11500L), 11499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(11501));\n\n        // new session\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 11600));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 11600L, 14600L), 14599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 10000));\n\n        // the maxTimestamp of the merged session is already late,\n        // so we get an immediate firing\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-7\", 1000L, 14600L), 14599));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>> sideActual =\n                testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 14500));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-8\", 1000L, 17500L), 17499));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(20000));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(100000));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(100000));\n\n        actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        sideActual = testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple3ResultSortComparator());\n        assertThat(sideActual).isNull();\n\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows2(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 100;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        String,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction2()),\n                                new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(lateness),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> testHarness =\n                enableAsyncState\n                        ? createAsyncTestHarness(builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction2()))\n                        : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2100));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(\"GOT: (key2,1)\", 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2100)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction2\n            implements WindowFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<String> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            out.collect(\"GOT: \" + Joiner.on(\",\").join(input));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new PassThroughFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new PassThroughWindowFunction<\n                                                String, TimeWindow, Tuple2<String, Integer>>()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncReduce(\n                                                new SumReducer(),\n                                                new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyListStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyReduceStateForSessionWindows() throws Exception {\n\n        final int gapSize = 3;\n        final long lateness = 10;\n\n        ReducingStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n                new ReducingStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        new SumReducer(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Tuple3<String, Long, Long>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(gapSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                stateDesc,\n                                new InternalSingleValueWindowFunction<>(\n                                        new ReducedSessionWindowFunction()),\n                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>\n                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key2\", 1), 1000));\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        expected.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple3<>(\"key2-1\", 1000L, 4000L), 3999));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(4998));\n\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(14600));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(14600));\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> actual = testHarness.getOutput();\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\", expected, actual, new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    @ParameterizedTest(name = \"Enable async state = {0}\")\n    @ValueSource(booleans = {false, true})\n    void testCleanupTimerWithEmptyStateNoResultForTumblingWindows(boolean enableAsyncState)\n            throws Exception {\n        final int windowSize = 2;\n        final long lateness = 1;\n\n        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> windowStateDesc =\n                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                        \"window-contents\",\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n        org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                windowStateDescV2 =\n                        new org.apache.flink.api.common.state.v2.ListStateDescriptor<>(\n                                \"window-contents\",\n                                STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n\n        WindowOperatorFactory<\n                        String,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n                        TimeWindow>\n                operator =\n                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n                                TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n                                windowStateDesc,\n                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new EmptyReturnFunction()),\n                                new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                                lateness,\n                                null /* late data output tag */);\n\n        WindowOperatorBuilder<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> builder =\n                new WindowOperatorBuilder<>(\n                        TumblingEventTimeWindows.of(Duration.ofSeconds(windowSize)),\n                        new FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger(),\n                        new ExecutionConfig(),\n                        STRING_INT_TUPLE,\n                        new TupleKeySelector(),\n                        TypeInformation.of(String.class));\n        builder.allowedLateness(Duration.ofMillis(lateness));\n\n        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n                testHarness =\n                        enableAsyncState\n                                ? createAsyncTestHarness(\n                                        builder.asyncApply(new EmptyReturnFunction()))\n                                : createTestHarness(operator);\n\n        testHarness.open();\n\n        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expected = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n        // normal element\n        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"test_key\", 1), 1000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1599));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1699));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1799));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(1999));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[(test_key,1)]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(2000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            // Be empty => list.get() is null\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n        if (enableAsyncState) {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((AsyncWindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .getProcessContext()\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDescV2)\n                                    .get())\n                    .isNull();\n        } else {\n            assertThat(\n                            ((WindowOperator<?, ?, ?, ?, ?>) testHarness.getOperator())\n                                    .processContext\n                                    .windowState()\n                                    .getListState(windowStateDesc)\n                                    .get()\n                                    .toString())\n                    .isEqualTo(\"[]\");\n        }\n\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1599));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1699));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1799));\n        expected.add(new Watermark(1999)); // here it fires and purges\n        expected.add(new Watermark(2000)); // here is the cleanup timer\n        expected.add(new Watermark(5000));\n\n        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n                \"Output was not correct.\",\n                expected,\n                testHarness.getOutput(),\n                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n        testHarness.close();\n    }\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    //  UDFs\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    private static class PassThroughFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> in : input) {\n                out.collect(in);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class EmptyReturnFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String k,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {}\n    }\n\n    private static class SumReducer implements ReduceFunction<Tuple2<String, Integer>> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public Tuple2<String, Integer> reduce(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> value1, Tuple2<String, Integer> value2) throws Exception {\n            return new Tuple2<>(value2.f0, value1.f1 + value2.f1);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class RichSumReducer<W extends Window>\n            extends RichWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>, String, W> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private boolean openCalled = false;\n\n        @Override\n        public void open(OpenContext openContext) throws Exception {\n            super.open(openContext);\n            openCalled = true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() throws Exception {\n            super.close();\n            closeCalled.incrementAndGet();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                W window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> input,\n                Collector<Tuple2<String, Integer>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n\n            assertThat(openCalled).as(\"Open was not called\").isTrue();\n\n            int sum = 0;\n\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> t : input) {\n                sum += t.f1;\n            }\n            out.collect(new Tuple2<>(key, sum));\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple2ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple2<String, Integer>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    return sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private static class Tuple3ResultSortComparator implements Comparator<Object>, Serializable {\n        @Override\n        public int compare(Object o1, Object o2) {\n            if (o1 instanceof Watermark || o2 instanceof Watermark) {\n                return 0;\n            } else {\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr0 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o1;\n                StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> sr1 =\n                        (StreamRecord<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>>) o2;\n                if (sr0.getTimestamp() != sr1.getTimestamp()) {\n                    return (int) (sr0.getTimestamp() - sr1.getTimestamp());\n                }\n                int comparison = sr0.getValue().f0.compareTo(sr1.getValue().f0);\n                if (comparison != 0) {\n                    return comparison;\n                } else {\n                    comparison = (int) (sr0.getValue().f1 - sr1.getValue().f1);\n                    if (comparison != 0) {\n                        return comparison;\n                    }\n                    return (int) (sr0.getValue().f2 - sr1.getValue().f2);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class TupleKeySelector implements KeySelector<Tuple2<String, Integer>, String> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public String getKey(Tuple2<String, Integer> value) throws Exception {\n            return value.f0;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedSessionWindowFunction\n            implements WindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void apply(\n                String key,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class SessionProcessWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            int sum = 0;\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> i : values) {\n                sum += i.f1;\n            }\n            String resultString = key + \"-\" + sum;\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            out.collect(new Tuple3<>(resultString, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class ReducedProcessSessionWindowFunction\n            extends ProcessWindowFunction<\n                    Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple3<String, Long, Long>, String, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        @Override\n        public void process(\n                String key,\n                Context context,\n                Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>> values,\n                Collector<Tuple3<String, Long, Long>> out)\n                throws Exception {\n            TimeWindow window = context.window();\n            for (Tuple2<String, Integer> val : values) {\n                out.collect(new Tuple3<>(key + \"-\" + val.f1, window.getStart(), window.getEnd()));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class PointSessionWindows extends EventTimeSessionWindows {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private PointSessionWindows(long sessionTimeout) {\n            super(sessionTimeout);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public Collection<TimeWindow> assignWindows(\n                Object element, long timestamp, WindowAssignerContext ctx) {\n            if (element instanceof Tuple2) {\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> t2 = (Tuple2<String, Integer>) element;\n                if (t2.f1 == 33) {\n                    return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp));\n                }\n            }\n            return Collections.singletonList(new TimeWindow(timestamp, timestamp + sessionTimeout));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A trigger that fires at the end of the window but does not purge the state of the fired\n     * window. This is to test the state garbage collection mechanism.\n     */\n    private static class EventTimeTriggerAccumGC extends Trigger<Object, TimeWindow> {\n        private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n        private long cleanupTime;\n\n        public EventTimeTriggerAccumGC(long cleanupTime) {\n            this.cleanupTime = cleanupTime;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Object element, long timestamp, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            if (window.maxTimestamp() <= ctx.getCurrentWatermark()) {\n                // if the watermark is already past the window fire immediately\n                return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n            } else {\n                ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n                return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) {\n            return time == window.maxTimestamp() || time == window.maxTimestamp() + cleanupTime\n                    ? TriggerResult.FIRE_AND_PURGE\n                    : TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.CONTINUE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {\n            ctx.deleteEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean canMerge() {\n            return true;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void onMerge(TimeWindow window, OnMergeContext ctx) {\n            ctx.registerEventTimeTimer(window.maxTimestamp());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"EventTimeTrigger()\";\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class FireEverytimeOnElementAndEventTimeTrigger\n            extends Trigger<Tuple2<String, Integer>, TimeWindow> {\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onElement(\n                Tuple2<String, Integer> element,\n                long timestamp,\n                TimeWindow window,\n                TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time, TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx)\n                throws Exception {\n            return TriggerResult.FIRE;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void clear(TimeWindow window, TriggerContext ctx) throws Exception {}\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

    {
      "lines": [
        "     @Test",
        "     void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {",
        "         // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)",
        "-        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements are properly sent to the side output",
        "-        // when using session windows with allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration",
        "+        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements that are too late are properly sent",
        "+        // to the side output and do NOT trigger merges when using session windows with",
        "+        // allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration.",
        "+        //",
        "+        // Critical scenario: An element that is too late (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)",
        "+        // arrives and COULD extend an existing session window, but should NOT be processed.",
        "+        // Instead, it must be sent to the side output WITHOUT triggering any merge.",
        " ",
        "-        final int sessionGap = 10; // 10 seconds gap between elements to create new session",
        "-        final long allowedLateness = 5; // Allow 5 seconds of lateness",
        "+        final int sessionGap = 3; // 3 seconds gap to create new session windows",
        "+        final long allowedLateness = 2000; // 2 seconds in milliseconds",
        " ",
        "         ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =",
        "                 new ListStateDescriptor<>("
      ],
      "newLines": 16,
      "newStart": 1159,
      "oldLines": 11,
      "oldStart": 1159
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "                                 stateDesc,",
        "                                 new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),",
        "                                 EventTimeTrigger.create(),",
        "-                                allowedLateness * 1000, // Convert to milliseconds",
        "+                                allowedLateness,",
        "                                 lateOutputTag);",
        " ",
        "         OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>"
      ],
      "newLines": 7,
      "newStart": 1191,
      "oldLines": 7,
      "oldStart": 1186
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();",
        " ",
        "-        // Create a session window:",
        "-        // Element 1 at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+10000] = [1000, 11000]",
        "-        // Element 2 at t=5000 falls within window [1000, 11000+10000-5000] range, causing merge",
        "-        // Final window is [1000, 15000] (5000 + 10000)",
        "+        // === Phase 1: Create and fire a session window ===",
        "+        // Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+3000] = [1000, 4000]",
        "         testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 5000));",
        " ",
        "-        // Advance watermark to 16000",
        "-        // Window maxTimestamp=15000, cleanup time=15000+5000=20000",
        "-        // Since watermark 16000 < cleanup time 20000, window is NOT cleaned up yet",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(16000));",
        "+        // Watermark to 5000: triggers window fire (window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 > 5000)",
        "+        // Window fires but is NOT cleaned up yet",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));",
        " ",
        "-        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 14999));",
        "-        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 14999));",
        "-        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(16000));",
        "+        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3999));",
        "+        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));",
        " ",
        "         TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(",
        "                 \"Output was not correct.\","
      ],
      "newLines": 16,
      "newStart": 1202,
      "oldLines": 21,
      "oldStart": 1197
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        "         testHarness.getOutput().clear();",
        " ",
        "-        // Advance watermark beyond cleanup time (>20000)",
        "-        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(21000));",
        "-        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(21000));",
        "+        // === Phase 2: Advance watermark to clean up the window ===",
        "+        // Watermark to 7000: now cleanup time = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 <= 7000, so window is cleaned up",
        "+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));",
        " ",
        "+        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7000));",

        "                 \"Output was not correct.\",",
        "                 expectedOutput,"
      ],

    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        "         testHarness.getOutput().clear();",
        " ",
        "-        // Send a late element at t=8000",
        "-        // isElementLate checks: 8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000? YES - it's late!",
        "-        // This late element should be sent to the side output",
        "-        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));",
        "+        // === Phase 3: Send a late element - critical test ===",
        "+        // Element at t=3000:",
        "+        // - isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = TRUE",
        "+        // - This element would have merged with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]",
        "+        // - But since it's late, it MUST be sent to side output WITHOUT merging",
        "+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));",
        " ",
        "-        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 3), 8000));",

        "+        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));",

        "         ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);",

46. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "new_string": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows - FIXED\n\n## Issue Analysis\n**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.\n\n**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:\n1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first\n2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements\n3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped\n4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded early lateness check for merging windows (lines 306-311 in WindowOperator.java):\n```java\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Don't merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // Original merge and process logic\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n## Test Scenario\n- Creates session window [1000, 4000]\n- Window fires at watermark=5000\n- Window cleaned up at watermark=7000\n- Late element arrives at t=3000 (late because 3000+2000 <= 7000)\n- Verifies element is sent to side output, not processed",
  "old_string": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows\n\n## Issue Analysis\n**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.\n\n**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:\n1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first\n2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements\n3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped\n4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted\n\n**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.\n\n## Key Files\n- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method\n- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic\n- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic\n\n## Implementation\n- Add early lateness check for merging windows\n- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late\n- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival",
  "replace_all": true
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "newString": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows - FIXED\n\n## Issue Analysis\n**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.\n\n**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:\n1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first\n2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements\n3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped\n4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded early lateness check for merging windows (lines 306-311 in WindowOperator.java):\n```java\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Don't merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // Original merge and process logic\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n## Test Scenario\n- Creates session window [1000, 4000]\n- Window fires at watermark=5000\n- Window cleaned up at watermark=7000\n- Late element arrives at t=3000 (late because 3000+2000 <= 7000)\n- Verifies element is sent to side output, not processed",
  "oldString": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows\n\n## Issue Analysis\n**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.\n\n**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:\n1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first\n2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements\n3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped\n4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted\n\n**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.\n\n## Key Files\n- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method\n- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic\n- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic\n\n## Implementation\n- Add early lateness check for merging windows\n- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late\n- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival",
  "originalFile": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows\n\n## Issue Analysis\n**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.\n\n**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:\n1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first\n2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements\n3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped\n4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`\n5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted\n\n**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.\n\n## Key Files\n- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method\n- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic\n- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic\n\n## Implementation\n- Add early lateness check for merging windows\n- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late\n- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "-# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows",
        "+# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows - FIXED",
        " ",
        " ## Issue Analysis",
        " **Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped."
      ],
      "newLines": 4,
      "newStart": 1,
      "oldLines": 4,
      "oldStart": 1
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        " 4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`",
        " 5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted",
        " ",
        "-**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.",
        "+## Solution Implemented",
        "+Added early lateness check for merging windows (lines 306-311 in WindowOperator.java):",
        "+```java",
        "+if (isElementLate(element)) {",
        "+    // Don't merge or process - skip directly",
        "+} else {",
        "+    for (W window : elementWindows) {",
        "+        // Original merge and process logic",
        "+    }",
        "+}",
        "+```",
        " ",
        "-## Key Files",
        "-- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method",
        "-- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic",
        "-- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic",
        "+## Files Modified",
        "+1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check",
        "+2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`",
        " ",
        "-## Implementation",
        "-- Add early lateness check for merging windows",
        "-- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late",
        "-- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival",
        "+## Test Scenario",
        "+- Creates session window [1000, 4000]",
        "+- Window fires at watermark=5000",
        "+- Window cleaned up at watermark=7000",
        "+- Late element arrives at t=3000 (late because 3000+2000 <= 7000)",
        "+- Verifies element is sent to side output, not processed"

{

  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "diff --git a/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java b/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\nindex 4bdc82c5..5d412160 100644\n--- a/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\n+++ b/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\n@@ -303,100 +303,107 @@ public class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n             MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n \n-            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n-\n-                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n-                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n-                // actualWindow == window\n-                W actualWindow =\n-                        mergingWindows.addWindow(\n-                                window,\n-                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n-                                    @Override\n-                                    public void merge(\n-                                            W mergeResult,\n-                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n-                                            W stateWindowResult,\n-                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n-                                            throws Exception {\n-\n-                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n-                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n-                                                        <= internalTimerService\n-                                                                .currentWatermark())) {\n-                                            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n-                                                    \"The end timestamp of an \"\n-                                                            + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n-                                                            + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n-                                                            + internalTimerService\n-                                                                    .currentWatermark()\n-                                                            + \" window: \"\n-                                                            + mergeResult);\n-                                        } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n-                                            long currentProcessingTime =\n-                                                    internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n-                                            if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n-                                                    <= currentProcessingTime) {\n+            // For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n+            // Late elements should not trigger merge operations\n+            if (isElementLate(element)) {\n+                // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n+                // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n+            } else {\n+                for (W window : elementWindows) {\n+\n+                    // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n+                    // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n+                    // actualWindow == window\n+                    W actualWindow =\n+                            mergingWindows.addWindow(\n+                                    window,\n+                                    new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n+                                        @Override\n+                                        public void merge(\n+                                                W mergeResult,\n+                                                Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n+                                                W stateWindowResult,\n+                                                Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n+                                                throws Exception {\n+\n+                                            if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n+                                                    && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n+                                                            <= internalTimerService\n+                                                                    .currentWatermark())) {\n                                                 throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n-                                                        \"The end timestamp of a \"\n-                                                                + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n-                                                                + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n-                                                                + currentProcessingTime\n+                                                        \"The end timestamp of an \"\n+                                                                + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n+                                                                + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n+                                                                + internalTimerService\n+                                                                        .currentWatermark()\n                                                                 + \" window: \"\n                                                                 + mergeResult);\n+                                            } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n+                                                long currentProcessingTime =\n+                                                        internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n+                                                if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n+                                                        <= currentProcessingTime) {\n+                                                    throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n+                                                            \"The end timestamp of a \"\n+                                                                    + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n+                                                                    + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n+                                                                    + currentProcessingTime\n+                                                                    + \" window: \"\n+                                                                    + mergeResult);\n+                                                }\n                                             }\n-                                        }\n \n-                                        triggerContext.key = key;\n-                                        triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n+                                            triggerContext.key = key;"
}
