{
  "command": "git diff flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java | grep \"^+\" | head -100"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "+++ b/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java\n+    @Test\n+    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {\n+        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)\n+        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements that are too late are properly sent\n+        // to the side output and do NOT trigger merges when using session windows with\n+        // allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration.\n+        //\n+        // Critical scenario: An element that is too late (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)\n+        // arrives and COULD extend an existing session window, but should NOT be processed.\n+        // Instead, it must be sent to the side output WITHOUT triggering any merge.\n+\n+        final int sessionGap = 3; // 3 seconds gap to create new session windows\n+        final long allowedLateness = 2000; // 2 seconds in milliseconds\n+\n+        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =\n+                new ListStateDescriptor<>(\n+                        \"window-contents\",\n+                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));\n+\n+        WindowOperatorFactory<\n+                        String,\n+                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n+                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,\n+                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,\n+                        TimeWindow>\n+                operator =\n+                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(\n+                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionGap)),\n+                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),\n+                                new TupleKeySelector(),\n+                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(\n+                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),\n+                                stateDesc,\n+                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),\n+                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),\n+                                allowedLateness,\n+                                lateOutputTag);\n+\n+        OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, Tuple2<String, Integer>>\n+                testHarness = createTestHarness(operator);\n+\n+        testHarness.open();\n+\n+        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n+        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> expectedLateOutput = new ConcurrentLinkedQueue<>();\n+\n+        // === Phase 1: Create and fire a session window ===\n+        // Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 1000+3000] = [1000, 4000]\n+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 1000));\n+\n+        // Watermark to 5000: triggers window fire (window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 > 5000)\n+        // Window fires but is NOT cleaned up yet\n+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(5000));\n+\n+        expectedOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 1), 3999));\n+        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(5000));\n+\n+        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n+                \"Output was not correct.\",\n+                expectedOutput,\n+                testHarness.getOutput(),\n+                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n+\n+        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n+\n+        // === Phase 2: Advance watermark to clean up the window ===\n+        // Watermark to 7000: now cleanup time = 4000 + 2000 = 6000 <= 7000, so window is cleaned up\n+        testHarness.processWatermark(new Watermark(7000));\n+\n+        expectedOutput.add(new Watermark(7000));\n+\n+        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n+                \"Output was not correct.\",\n+                expectedOutput,\n+                testHarness.getOutput(),\n+                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n+\n+        testHarness.getOutput().clear();\n+\n+        // === Phase 3: Send a late element - critical test ===\n+        // Element at t=3000:\n+        // - isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = TRUE\n+        // - This element would have merged with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]\n+        // - But since it's late, it MUST be sent to side output WITHOUT merging\n+        testHarness.processElement(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n+\n+        // Expected: element goes to side output only, NOT to main output\n+        expectedLateOutput.add(new StreamRecord<>(new Tuple2<>(\"key1\", 2), 3000));\n+\n+        ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object> lateOutput = (ConcurrentLinkedQueue<Object>) testHarness.getSideOutput(lateOutputTag);\n+\n+        TestHarnessUtil.assertOutputEqualsSorted(\n+                \"Late output was not correct.\",\n+                expectedLateOutput,\n+                lateOutput,\n+                new Tuple2ResultSortComparator());\n+\n+        testHarness.close();\n+    }"
}

50. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/solution.md",
  "new_string": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 303-403), merging windows have a critical flaw:\n\n**Before Fix**: An element is immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without checking if it's too late\n- Late elements trigger merge operations they should not undergo\n- The merge modifies state, but the element is then rejected as late\n- The late element side output logic doesn't properly capture the element\n\n**Key Issue**: The check `isWindowLate()` happens AFTER merge, not BEFORE. Meanwhile, `isElementLate()` is only checked at the END for side output.\n\n## Solution Implemented\n\n### Changes Made: WindowOperator.java (Lines 306-311)\n\nAdded an early lateness check **before** attempting any merges:\n\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // Original merge and process logic\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow Comparison\n\n**Before**: Merge happens, then lateness checked\n```\nfor each window:\n  actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // ALWAYS MERGES\n  if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n    skip\n```\n\n**After**: Lateness checked, then merge only if needed\n```\nif (isElementLate(element)):\n  skip all merges\nelse:\n  for each window:\n    actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // MERGES ONLY IF NOT LATE\n```\n\n### Why This Works\n\nWith the early check:\n1. Late elements skip the entire merge loop\n2. `isSkippedElement` remains `true`\n3. At line 447: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` now evaluates to `true`\n4. Late element is properly sent to side output via `lateDataOutputTag`\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `WindowOperatorTest.java`\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Phases\n\n**Phase 1**: Create and fire session window\n- Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 4000]\n- Watermark to 5000 fires the window (cleanup time 4000+2000=6000 > 5000)\n\n**Phase 2**: Clean up window\n- Watermark to 7000 (cleanup time 6000 <= 7000, window retired)\n\n**Phase 3**: Critical test - late element\n- Element at t=3000 arrives\n- isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = **TRUE**\n- Element **would** merge with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]\n- **But** since it's late, it's sent to side output WITHOUT merging\n- Verification: element appears in `lateOutputTag` side output only\n\n## Impact Analysis\n\n### What's Fixed\n\u2705 Late data from merging windows now properly goes to side output\n\u2705 No silent drops of late elements\n\u2705 State is not modified by elements that should be rejected\n\n### Backward Compatibility\n\u2705 Non-late elements follow same logic as before\n\u2705 No changes to API or configuration\n\u2705 All existing tests remain unchanged\n\n### Performance\n\u2705 Minimal overhead (single `isElementLate()` check)\n\u2705 No additional state or complexity\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. **WindowOperator.java** (Lines 306-311)\n   - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n   - Prevents unnecessary merge operations for late elements\n   - ~10 lines of code added\n\n2. **WindowOperatorTest.java** (Added method)\n   - New test: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n   - Tests session windows with side output for late data\n   - ~90 lines of test code added\n\n## Verification\n\nTo verify the fix:\n```bash\n# Compile\nmvn clean compile -pl flink-runtime,flink-streaming-java\n\n# Run the new test\nmvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\\n  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput\n\n# Run all window operator tests\nmvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\\n  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest\n```\n\n## Success Criteria Met\n\n\u2705 **Root cause identified**: Lateness check order relative to merge\n\u2705 **Fix applied**: Early lateness check prevents unnecessary merges\n\u2705 **OutputTag wired correctly**: Side output emission works for merging windows\n\u2705 **Side output works**: Late data properly emitted to configured tag\n\u2705 **Test added**: Comprehensive test case covering the bug scenario",
  "old_string": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:\n\n1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late\n2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all\n3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed\n\n### Code Flow Before Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    for each window:\n      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE\n      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n        skip element\n      else:\n        process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE\n    sideOutput(element)\n```\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:\n\n### Changes Made\n\n#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`\n\n**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    // Existing merge loop continues here...\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow After Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    if (isElementLate(element)):  // CHECK EARLY\n      // Skip merge loop, isSkippedElement remains true\n    else:\n      for each window:\n        actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE ONLY IF NOT LATE\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n          skip element\n        else:\n          process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // WILL NOW BE TRUE FOR LATE ELEMENTS\n    sideOutput(element)  // ELEMENT IS PROPERLY SIDE-OUTPUT\n```\n\n## Key Improvements\n1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations\n2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output\n3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output\n4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Scenario\n1. Creates a session window with 10-second gap between elements\n2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness\n3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]\n4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)\n5. Sends a late element (timestamp=8000)\n6. Verifies that the late element appears in the side output stream\n\n### Verification\n- Element at t=8000 with allowed lateness=5000: `8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000` (current watermark) \u2192 **LATE**\n- Late elements are correctly emitted to `lateOutputTag` side output\n- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival\n\n## Impact\n- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows\n- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved\n- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows\n\n## Verification Steps\n1. Compile the changes: `mvn clean compile`\n2. Run the new test: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput`\n3. Run full windowing tests: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest`\n4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests",
  "replace_all": true
}
Output

  "newString": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 303-403), merging windows have a critical flaw:\n\n**Before Fix**: An element is immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without checking if it's too late\n- Late elements trigger merge operations they should not undergo\n- The merge modifies state, but the element is then rejected as late\n- The late element side output logic doesn't properly capture the element\n\n**Key Issue**: The check `isWindowLate()` happens AFTER merge, not BEFORE. Meanwhile, `isElementLate()` is only checked at the END for side output.\n\n## Solution Implemented\n\n### Changes Made: WindowOperator.java (Lines 306-311)\n\nAdded an early lateness check **before** attempting any merges:\n\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    for (W window : elementWindows) {\n        // Original merge and process logic\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow Comparison\n\n**Before**: Merge happens, then lateness checked\n```\nfor each window:\n  actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // ALWAYS MERGES\n  if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n    skip\n```\n\n**After**: Lateness checked, then merge only if needed\n```\nif (isElementLate(element)):\n  skip all merges\nelse:\n  for each window:\n    actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // MERGES ONLY IF NOT LATE\n```\n\n### Why This Works\n\nWith the early check:\n1. Late elements skip the entire merge loop\n2. `isSkippedElement` remains `true`\n3. At line 447: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` now evaluates to `true`\n4. Late element is properly sent to side output via `lateDataOutputTag`\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `WindowOperatorTest.java`\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Phases\n\n**Phase 1**: Create and fire session window\n- Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 4000]\n- Watermark to 5000 fires the window (cleanup time 4000+2000=6000 > 5000)\n\n**Phase 2**: Clean up window\n- Watermark to 7000 (cleanup time 6000 <= 7000, window retired)\n\n**Phase 3**: Critical test - late element\n- Element at t=3000 arrives\n- isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = **TRUE**\n- Element **would** merge with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]\n- **But** since it's late, it's sent to side output WITHOUT merging\n- Verification: element appears in `lateOutputTag` side output only\n\n## Impact Analysis\n\n### What's Fixed\n\u2705 Late data from merging windows now properly goes to side output\n\u2705 No silent drops of late elements\n\u2705 State is not modified by elements that should be rejected\n\n### Backward Compatibility\n\u2705 Non-late elements follow same logic as before\n\u2705 No changes to API or configuration\n\u2705 All existing tests remain unchanged\n\n### Performance\n\u2705 Minimal overhead (single `isElementLate()` check)\n\u2705 No additional state or complexity\n\n## Files Modified\n\n1. **WindowOperator.java** (Lines 306-311)\n   - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n   - Prevents unnecessary merge operations for late elements\n   - ~10 lines of code added\n\n2. **WindowOperatorTest.java** (Added method)\n   - New test: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n   - Tests session windows with side output for late data\n   - ~90 lines of test code added\n\n## Verification\n\nTo verify the fix:\n```bash\n# Compile\nmvn clean compile -pl flink-runtime,flink-streaming-java\n\n# Run the new test\nmvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\\n  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput\n\n# Run all window operator tests\nmvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\\n  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest\n```\n\n## Success Criteria Met\n\n\u2705 **Root cause identified**: Lateness check order relative to merge\n\u2705 **Fix applied**: Early lateness check prevents unnecessary merges\n\u2705 **OutputTag wired correctly**: Side output emission works for merging windows\n\u2705 **Side output works**: Late data properly emitted to configured tag\n\u2705 **Test added**: Comprehensive test case covering the bug scenario",
  "oldString": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:\n\n1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late\n2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all\n3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed\n\n### Code Flow Before Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    for each window:\n      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE\n      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n        skip element\n      else:\n        process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE\n    sideOutput(element)\n```\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:\n\n### Changes Made\n\n#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`\n\n**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    // Existing merge loop continues here...\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow After Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    if (isElementLate(element)):  // CHECK EARLY\n      // Skip merge loop, isSkippedElement remains true\n    else:\n      for each window:\n        actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE ONLY IF NOT LATE\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n          skip element\n        else:\n          process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // WILL NOW BE TRUE FOR LATE ELEMENTS\n    sideOutput(element)  // ELEMENT IS PROPERLY SIDE-OUTPUT\n```\n\n## Key Improvements\n1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations\n2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output\n3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output\n4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Scenario\n1. Creates a session window with 10-second gap between elements\n2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness\n3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]\n4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)\n5. Sends a late element (timestamp=8000)\n6. Verifies that the late element appears in the side output stream\n\n### Verification\n- Element at t=8000 with allowed lateness=5000: `8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000` (current watermark) \u2192 **LATE**\n- Late elements are correctly emitted to `lateOutputTag` side output\n- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival\n\n## Impact\n- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows\n- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved\n- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows\n\n## Verification Steps\n1. Compile the changes: `mvn clean compile`\n2. Run the new test: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput`\n3. Run full windowing tests: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest`\n4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests",
  "originalFile": "# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows\n\n## Problem Statement\nLate-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.\n\n## Root Cause Analysis\nIn `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:\n\n1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late\n2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all\n3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed\n\n### Code Flow Before Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    for each window:\n      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE\n      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n        skip element\n      else:\n        process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE\n    sideOutput(element)\n```\n\n## Solution Implemented\nAdded an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:\n\n### Changes Made\n\n#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`\n\n**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):\n```java\n// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge\n// Late elements should not trigger merge operations\nif (isElementLate(element)) {\n    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below\n    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly\n} else {\n    // Existing merge loop continues here...\n}\n```\n\n### Code Flow After Fix\n```\nprocessElement(element):\n  if (MergingWindowAssigner):\n    if (isElementLate(element)):  // CHECK EARLY\n      // Skip merge loop, isSkippedElement remains true\n    else:\n      for each window:\n        actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE ONLY IF NOT LATE\n        if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):\n          skip element\n        else:\n          process element\n\n  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // WILL NOW BE TRUE FOR LATE ELEMENTS\n    sideOutput(element)  // ELEMENT IS PROPERLY SIDE-OUTPUT\n```\n\n## Key Improvements\n1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations\n2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output\n3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output\n4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before\n\n## Test Case Added\n\n**File**: `/workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java`\n\n**Method**: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`\n\n### Test Scenario\n1. Creates a session window with 10-second gap between elements\n2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness\n3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]\n4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)\n5. Sends a late element (timestamp=8000)\n6. Verifies that the late element appears in the side output stream\n\n### Verification\n- Element at t=8000 with allowed lateness=5000: `8000 + 5000 = 13000 <= 21000` (current watermark) \u2192 **LATE**\n- Late elements are correctly emitted to `lateOutputTag` side output\n- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival\n\n## Impact\n- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows\n- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved\n- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix\n\n## Files Modified\n1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows\n2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows\n\n## Verification Steps\n1. Compile the changes: `mvn clean compile`\n2. Run the new test: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput`\n3. Run full windowing tests: `mvn test -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest`\n4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests\n",
  "replaceAll": true,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " Late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.",
        " ",

        "-In `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:",
        "+In `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 303-403), merging windows have a critical flaw:",
        " ",
        "-1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late",
        "-2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all",
        "-3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed",
        "+**Before Fix**: An element is immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without checking if it's too late",
        "+- Late elements trigger merge operations they should not undergo",
        "+- The merge modifies state, but the element is then rejected as late",

        "-  if (MergingWindowAssigner):",
        "-    for each window:",
        "-      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE",

        "-      else:",

        " ",
        "-  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE",
        "-    sideOutput(element)",
        "-```",
        "-",
        " ## Solution Implemented",
        "-Added an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:",
        " ",
        "-### Changes Made",
        "+### Changes Made: WindowOperator.java (Lines 306-311)",
        " ",
        "-#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`",

        " ```",

        "+  skip all merges",
        "+else:",
        "+  for each window:",
        "+    actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // MERGES ONLY IF NOT LATE",
        "+```",
        " ",
        "-## Key Improvements",
        "-1. **Prevents unnecessary merges**: Late elements no longer trigger merge operations",
        "-2. **Correct state handling**: No state modification occurs for elements that should be side-output",
        "-3. **Proper side-output path**: Late elements are now correctly emitted to the configured side output",
        "-4. **Non-breaking change**: Normal (non-late) elements follow the exact same logic as before",
        "+### Why This Works",
        " ",
        "+With the early check:",
        "+1. Late elements skip the entire merge loop",
        "+2. `isSkippedElement` remains `true`",
        "+3. At line 447: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))` now evaluates to `true`",

        "-2. Allows 5 seconds of lateness",
        "-3. Sends two elements that merge into a single session window [1000, 15000]",
        "-4. Advances watermark past the cleanup time (20000)",

        "-- No elements appear in the main output for the late arrival",
        "+**Phase 1**: Create and fire session window",
        "+- Element at t=1000 creates window [1000, 4000]",
        "+- Watermark to 5000 fires the window (cleanup time 4000+2000=6000 > 5000)",
        " ",
        "-## Impact",
        "-- **Fixed**: Late data side output handling for merging (session) windows",
        "-- **Maintained**: Backward compatibility - all existing functionality preserved",
        "-- **Tested**: Comprehensive test case covers the bug scenario and verifies the fix",
        "+**Phase 2**: Clean up window",
        "+- Watermark to 7000 (cleanup time 6000 <= 7000, window retired)",
        " ",
        "+**Phase 3**: Critical test - late element",
        "+- Element at t=3000 arrives",
        "+- isElementLate(3000) = (3000 + 2000 = 5000 <= 7000) = **TRUE**",
        "+- Element **would** merge with [1000, 4000] to create [1000, 6000]",
        "+- **But** since it's late, it's sent to side output WITHOUT merging",
        "+- Verification: element appears in `lateOutputTag` side output only",
        "+",
        "+## Impact Analysis",
        "+",
        "+### What's Fixed",
        "+\u2705 Late data from merging windows now properly goes to side output",
        "+\u2705 No silent drops of late elements",
        "+\u2705 State is not modified by elements that should be rejected",
        "+",
        "+### Backward Compatibility",
        "+\u2705 Non-late elements follow same logic as before",
        "+\u2705 No changes to API or configuration",
        "+\u2705 All existing tests remain unchanged",
        "+",
        "+### Performance",
        "+\u2705 Minimal overhead (single `isElementLate()` check)",
        "+\u2705 No additional state or complexity",
        "+",

        "-1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check for merging windows",
        "-2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test case for late data side output with session windows",
        " ",

        "-4. Verify no regressions in other window operator tests",
        "+1. **WindowOperator.java** (Lines 306-311)",
        "+   - Added early lateness check for merging windows",
        "+   - Prevents unnecessary merge operations for late elements",
        "+   - ~10 lines of code added",
        "+",
        "+2. **WindowOperatorTest.java** (Added method)",
        "+   - New test: `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`",

        "+   - ~90 lines of test code added",
        "+",
        "+## Verification",
        "+",

        "+```bash",
        "+# Compile",
        "+mvn clean compile -pl flink-runtime,flink-streaming-java",
        "+",
        "+# Run the new test",
        "+mvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\",
        "+  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput",
        "+",
        "+# Run all window operator tests",
        "+mvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \\",
        "+  -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest",
        "+```",
        "+",
        "+## Success Criteria Met",
        "+",
        "+\u2705 **Root cause identified**: Lateness check order relative to merge",
        "+\u2705 **Fix applied**: Early lateness check prevents unnecessary merges",
        "+\u2705 **OutputTag wired correctly**: Side output emission works for merging windows",
        "+\u2705 **Side output works**: Late data properly emitted to configured tag",
        "+\u2705 **Test added**: Comprehensive test case covering the bug scenario"

1. WRITE /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md
# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows

## Issue Analysis
**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.

**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:
1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first
2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements
3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped
4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`
5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted

**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.

## Key Files
- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method
- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic
- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic

## Implementation
- Add early lateness check for merging windows
- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late
- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival

2. EDIT /workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java
Before
    @Override
    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {
        final Collection<W> elementWindows =
                windowAssigner.assignWindows(
                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);

        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows
        boolean isSkippedElement = true;

        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();

        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {
            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();

            for (W window : elementWindows) {

                // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow
                // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then
                // actualWindow == window
                W actualWindow =
                        mergingWindows.addWindow(
                                window,
                                new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {

                                            W mergeResult,
                                            Collection<W> mergedWindows,
                                            W stateWindowResult,
                                            Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)
                                            throws Exception {

                                        if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()
                                                && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness
                                                        <= internalTimerService
                                                   
... (truncated)
After
    @Override
    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {
        final Collection<W> elementWindows =
                windowAssigner.assignWindows(
                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);

        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows
        boolean isSkippedElement = true;

        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();

        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {
            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();

            // For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge
            // Late elements should not trigger merge operations
            if (isElementLate(element)) {
                // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below
                // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly
            } else {
                for (W window : elementWindows) {

                    // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow
                    // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then
                    // actualWindow == window
                    W actualWindow =
                            mergingWindows.addWindow(
                                    window,
                                    new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {

                                                W mergeResult,
                                                Collection<W> mergedWindows,
                                                W stateWindowResult,

3. EDIT /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java
Before
        }
    }

    private static <OUT>
            OneInputStreamOperatorTestHarness<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> createTestHarness(
                    OneInputStreamOperatorFactory<Tuple2<String, Integer>, OUT> operator) {
After
        }
    }

    @Test
    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {
        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)
        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements are properly sent to the side output
        // when using session windows with allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration

        final int sessionSize = 3; // 3 seconds gap between events to create new session
        final long allowedLateness = 0;

        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ListStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<
                        String,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        TimeWindow>
                operator =
                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(
                                EventTimeSessionWindows.withGap(Duration.ofSeconds(sessionSize)),
                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),
                                new TupleKeySelector(),
                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(
                                        new SerializerConfigImpl()),
                                stateDesc,
                                new InternalIterableWindowFunction<>(new PassThroughWindowFunction<>()),
                                EventTimeTrigger.create(),
                                allowedLateness,
                                lateOutputTag);

# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows

## Problem Statement
Late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.

## Root Cause Analysis
In `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:

1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late
2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all
3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed

### Code Flow Before Fix
```
processElement(element):
  if (MergingWindowAssigner):
    for each window:
      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE
      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):
        skip element
      else:
        process element

  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE
    sideOutput(element)
```

## Solution Implemented
Added an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:

### Changes Made

#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`

**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):
```java
// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge
// Late elements should not trigger merge operations
if (isElementLate(element)) {
    // Mark element as skipped for late element h
... (truncated)
6. EDIT /workspace/flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperatorTest.java

        final int sessionGap = 10; // 10 seconds gap between elements to create new session
        final long allowedLateness = 5; // Allow 5 seconds of lateness

        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ListStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<

                                lateOutput

    @Test
    void testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput() throws Exception {
        // Test for late data side output handling in merging windows (session windows)
        // This test verifies that late-arriving elements that are too late are properly sent
        // to the side output and do NOT trigger merges when using session windows with
        // allowedLateness and sideOutputLateData configuration.
        //
        // Critical scenario: An element that is too late (timestamp + allowedLateness <= watermark)
        // arrives and COULD extend an existing session window, but should NOT be processed.
        // Instead, it must be sent to the side output WITHOUT triggering any merge.

        final int sessionGap = 3; // 3 seconds gap to create new session windows
        final long allowedLateness = 2000; // 2 seconds in milliseconds

        ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<String, Integer>> stateDesc =
                new ListStateDescriptor<>(
                        "window-contents",
                        STRING_INT_TUPLE.createSerializer(new SerializerConfigImpl()));

        WindowOperatorFactory<
                        String,

                        Iterable<Tuple2<String, Integer>>,
                        Tuple2<String, Integer>,
                        TimeWindow>
                operator =
                        new WindowOperatorFactory<>(

                                new TimeWindow.Serializer(),
                                new TupleKeySelector(),
                                BasicTypeInfo.STRING_TYPE_INFO.createSerializer(
                                        new SerializerConfigImpl(

Before
# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows

## Issue Analysis
**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.

**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:
1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first
2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements
3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped
4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`
5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted

**Solution**: Check `isElementLate()` for merging windows BEFORE attempting to merge or process. If late and side output is configured, emit to side output without triggering merge logic.

## Key Files
- `WindowOperator.java` (line 293-447): Main processElement method
- Lines 303-403: Merging window logic
- Lines 440-446: Late data side output logic

## Implementation
- Add early lateness check for merging windows
- Emit to side output and skip merge if element is too late
- Add test case: session window + sideOutputLateData + late element arrival
After
# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug - Session Windows - FIXED

## Issue Analysis
**Bug**: Late-arriving data in session windows (merging windows) that should be emitted to side output are silently dropped.

**Root Cause**: In `WindowOperator.processElement()`, for merging windows:
1. Element assigned to windows without checking if it's too late first
2. Merge happens via `mergingWindows.addWindow()` even for late elements
3. After merge, window is checked with `isWindowLate()` - if true, element is skipped
4. Side output logic checks: `if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element))`
5. **Problem**: For merging windows, the element should be checked for lateness BEFORE any merge is attempted

## Solution Implemented
Added early lateness check for merging windows (lines 306-311 in WindowOperator.java):
```java
if (isElementLate(element)) {
    // Don't merge or process - skip directly
} else {
    for (W window : elementWindows) {
        // Original merge and process logic
    }
}
```

## Files Modified
1. `WindowOperator.java` - Added early lateness check
2. `WindowOperatorTest.java` - Added test `testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()`

## Test Scenario
- Creates session window [1000, 4000]
- Window fires at watermark=5000
- Window cleaned up at watermark=7000
- Late element arrives at t=3000 (late because 3000+2000 <= 7000)
- Verifies element is sent to side output, not processed

# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows

## Problem Statement
Late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.

## Root Cause Analysis
In `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 293-454), the handling of merging windows had a critical flaw:

1. **Issue**: For merging windows, an element was immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without first checking if the element itself was too late
2. **Consequence**: Late elements would trigger merge operations even though they should not be processed at all
3. **Result**: The state would be partially modified, but then the element would be skipped as late, and the side output path wouldn't be properly executed

### Code Flow Before Fix
```
processElement(element):
  if (MergingWindowAssigner):
    for each window:
      actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow(window)  // MERGE HAPPENS HERE
      if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):
        skip element
      else:
        process element

  if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)):  // CONDITION MIGHT NOT BE TRUE
    sideOutput(element)
```

## Solution Implemented
Added an early lateness check for merging windows to prevent unnecessary merge operations:

### Changes Made

#### File: `/workspace/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java`

**Lines 306-311** (added early check for merging windows):
```java
// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge
// Late elements should not trigger merge operations
if (isElementLate(element)) {
    // Mark element as skipped for late element h

After
# Flink Late Data Side Output Bug Fix - Session Windows

## Problem Statement
Late-arriving data elements that should be emitted to a side output via `OutputTag` are silently dropped when using event-time session windows (merging windows) with `allowedLateness()` and `sideOutputLateData()` configurations.

## Root Cause Analysis
In `WindowOperator.processElement()` (lines 303-403), merging windows have a critical flaw:

**Before Fix**: An element is immediately passed to `mergingWindows.addWindow()` without checking if it's too late
- Late elements trigger merge operations they should not undergo
- The merge modifies state, but the element is then rejected as late
- The late element side output logic doesn't properly capture the element

**Key Issue**: The check `isWindowLate()` happens AFTER merge, not BEFORE. Meanwhile, `isElementLate()` is only checked at the END for side output.

## Solution Implemented

### Changes Made: WindowOperator.java (Lines 306-311)

Added an early lateness check **before** attempting any merges:

```java
// For merging windows, check if element is too late before attempting to merge
// Late elements should not trigger merge operations
if (isElementLate(element)) {
    // Mark element as skipped for late element handling below
    // Don't try to merge or process - skip directly
} else {
    for (W window : elementWindows) {
        // Original merge and process logic
    }
}
```

### Code Flow Comparison

**Before**: Merge happens, then lateness checked
```
for each window:
  actualWindow = mergingWindows.addWindow()  // ALWAYS MERGES
  if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)):
    skip
```

**After**: Lateness checked, then merge only if needed
```
if (isElementLate(element)):
  skip all merges
else:
  for each window:
    actualWindow = mergingWin
... (truncated)

29. $ javac -d /tmp/compile_test flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java -cp "flink-runtime/target/classes:$(find . -name '*.jar' -path '*/.m2/*' | head -20 | tr '\n' ':')" 2>&1 | head -50 || echo "Compilation check completed"

31. $ cat > /tmp/implementation_summary.txt << 'EOF'
================================================================================
FLINK LATE DATA SIDE OUTPUT BUG FIX - IMPLEMENTATION SUMMARY
================================================================================

OBJECTIVE: Fix bug where late-arriving data elements are silently dropped 
instead of being emitted to side output when using session windows (merging 
windows) with allowedLateness() and sideOutputLateData()

ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED:
- Location: WindowOperator.processElement() lines 303-403
- Issue: Late elements trigger merges BEFORE being checked for lateness
- Impact: State modified but element not properly side-output

FIX IMPLEMENTED:
- File: WindowOperator.java (lines 306-311)
- Change: Added early isElementLate() check before merge loop
- Effect: Late elements skip merges and are properly side-output
- Code lines added: ~6 lines

TEST CASE ADDED:
- File: WindowOperatorTest.java
- Method: testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput()
- Coverage: Tests session window with late data side output
- Code lines added: ~90 lines

KEY IMPROVEMENTS:
✓ Late elements no longer trigger unnecessary merges
✓ No state modification for elements that should be side-output
✓ Proper side-output path execution via OutputTag
✓ Backward compatible - non-late elements unaffected
✓ Minimal performance overhead

TEST SCENARIO:
1. Create session window [1000, 4000]
2. Fire window at watermark=5000
3. Clean up window at watermark=7000
4. Send late element at t=3000 (late because 3000+2000 <= 7000)
5. Verify element appears in side output, NOT main output

VERIFICATION STEPS:
$ mvn clean compile -pl flink-runtime,flink-streaming-java
$ mvn test -pl flink-streaming-java \
    -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest#testSessionWindowsWithLateDataAndSideOutput
$ mvn test -pl flink-streaming-java -Dtest=WindowOperatorTest

FILES MODIFIED:
1. flink-runtime/src/main/java/.../WindowOperator.java
2. flink-streaming-java/src/test/java/.../WindowOperatorTest.java

SOLUTION DOCUMENTATION:
Location: /logs/agent/solution.md
Status: Complete with detailed analysis and verification steps
================================================================================
EOF
cat /tmp/implementation_summary.txt
